title: Ducommun Incorporated Reports First Quarter 2026 Results
author: Na
description: Ducommun Incorporated reported Q1 2026 net revenue of $209.0 million, up 9% year-over-year, with net income of $9.9 million (up 607% YoY) and adjusted EBITDA of $35.4 million (up 19% YoY). The company achieved gross margin expansion of 70 basis points to 26.9% and adjusted EBITDA margin of 16.9%, driven by strong commercial aerospace growth (18% YoY) and solid defense business performance. Management expressed confidence in meeting VISION 2027 financial goals despite anticipated destocking headwinds in remaining 2026 quarters.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Referenced as a defense prime with long-term 7-year framework agreements for key missile programs. Ducommun is positioned to benefit from these agreements, indicating a positive business relationship and growth opportunity.

Strong Q1 2026 results with 9% revenue growth, 607% net income increase, and 70 bps gross margin expansion. Commercial aerospace segment showed robust 18% YoY growth, defense business continued to grow, and adjusted EBITDA margins expanded 150 bps to 16.9%. Management expressed high confidence in achieving VISION 2027 goals with seven quarters remaining. Operating cash flow improved significantly to $11.2 million from $0.8 million YoY.

title: Meros Investment Management Sold 396,804 Shares of Photronics Stock
author: John Ballard
description: Meros Investment Management reduced its stake in Photronics by 396,804 shares in Q4 2025, selling approximately $10 million worth. The fund retained 108,004 shares valued at $3.4 million, representing 1.4% of AUM. The sale reflects a value investing strategy to lock in gains after Photronics' strong 74.2% annual performance, while the fund reallocated capital to other undervalued holdings.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: While Photronics showed strong performance (74.2% annual gain), Meros sold shares to lock in gains, suggesting the fund views the stock as fairly valued rather than undervalued. This is a profit-taking move rather than a bearish signal.
Listed as Meros' top holding at 6.6% of AUM (~$16.6 million), indicating strong conviction and continued confidence in the position.
Meros was actively buying Magnite in Q4 2025 while it was trading down, indicating the fund sees undervalued opportunity and conviction in future upside.
Meros reduced its stake in Thermon alongside Photronics after strong outperformance, suggesting a similar profit-taking strategy rather than negative outlook.
Meros was buying Amplitude in Q4 2025 while it traded down, indicating the fund believes it is undervalued with more upside potential.

title: Defense Stocks Hit Records As Trump Warns 'Big Wave' In Iran: 10 Names In Focus
author: Piero Cingari
description: Aerospace and defense stocks surged to record levels as Trump indicated a sustained military campaign against Iran is ahead, with the most intense phase still to come. Defense leadership confirmed the operation will extend beyond initial strikes, driving market expectations for increased demand for advanced weapons systems, air defense, and surveillance technologies.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock declined 11.2% despite broader defense sector rally, suggesting underperformance relative to peers in the defense sector surge

title: Ducommun Incorporated Announces Fourth Quarter Conference Call
author: Na
description: Ducommun Incorporated announced it will release its 2025 fourth quarter financial results on February 26, 2026, with a conference call at 10:00 a.m. PT featuring CEO Stephen G. Oswald and CFO Suman Mookerji to discuss results and answer questions.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article is a routine earnings announcement with no new business developments, financial performance data, or forward-looking statements that would indicate positive or negative sentiment. It is a standard procedural announcement of when results will be released.

title: Why One Fund Cut a $4.1 Million Stake Amid Interface’s Blowout $364.5 Million Quarter
author: Jonathan Ponciano
description: Meros Investment Management sold 259,973 shares of Interface in Q3, reducing its stake by $4.1 million, while the company reported strong quarterly results with 5.9% revenue growth and raised full-year guidance.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong quarterly performance with 5.9% revenue growth, 62.5% EPS surge, raised full-year guidance, and robust cash generation of $76.7 million
